content.text:
1	# PetMarket — Claude Code Project Context
2	
3	## Project Overview
4	Full-stack offline pet marketplace. NestJS backend + React frontend + PostgreSQL.
5	Monorepo structure. Fully Dockerized. No external services or APIs.
6	
7	## Monorepo Structure
8	```
9	petmarket/
10	├── backend/          # NestJS, TypeScript, TypeORM, PostgreSQL
11	├── frontend/         # React 18, Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui
12	├── unit_tests/       # Jest unit tests
13	├── API_tests/        # curl-based API tests
14	├── docker-compose.yml
15	├── run_tests.sh
16	└── README.md
17	```
18	
19	## Tech Stack
20	### Backend
21	- NestJS + TypeScript
22	- TypeORM + PostgreSQL 16
23	- class-validator + class-transformer (all DTOs)
24	- bcrypt (password hashing)
25	- JWT (auth)
26	- AES-256-CBC (encrypt sensitive fields at rest)
27	- Winston (logging)
28	- Jest (unit tests)
29	
30	### Frontend
31	- React 18 + Vite + TypeScript
32	- TailwindCSS v3 + shadcn/ui
33	- TanStack React Query (all data fetching)
34	- React Router v6
35	- Zustand (auth store)
36	- Axios (global instance with auth interceptor)
37	- React Hook Form + Zod (all forms)
38	
39	## Architecture Rules — NEVER violate these
40	
41	### Backend
42	- Strict layering: Controller → Service → Repository. No DB calls in controllers. No business logic in controllers.
43	- Every endpoint wrapped in `{ code, msg, data }` response envelope.
44	- Every error returns `{ code: number, msg: string, timestamp: string }` — never raw stack traces.
45	- All user inputs validated via DTOs with class-validator before reaching service layer.
46	- All mutating endpoints protected by JwtAuthGuard + RolesGuard.
47	- Audit log every create/update/delete via AuditService (hash-chained, append-only).
48	- No raw SQL string concatenation — always TypeORM QueryBuilder or Repository methods.
49	
50	### Frontend
51	- No "God components" — split logically, max ~150 lines per component.
52	- All API calls go through typed functions in `/src/api/` — never inline fetch/axios in components.
53	- All async data uses React Query (loading skeleton + error state with retry on every data-fetching component).
54	- All forms use React Hook Form + Zod. Show inline field errors. Disable + spinner on submit.
55	- Toast notifications (top-right) for every API success and error response.
56	- ProtectedRoute wraps all authenticated pages — redirects to /login if no token or wrong role.
57	
58	## Roles
59	- `shopper` — browse listings, start conversations, view own credit score
60	- `vendor` — manage own listings, handle conversations, view settlements
61	- `admin` — full access: config, campaigns, settlements approval, audit, exports, query
62	- `ops_reviewer` — step 1 of settlement approval only
63	- `finance_admin` — step 2 of settlement approval only
64	
65	## Key Business Rules (never simplify these)
66	- Sensitive word filter: blocks publish → sets status=pending_review + sensitiveWordFlagged=true
67	- Rate limits: 30 listings/hour/vendor; 10 new conversations/10min/account → HTTP 429
68	- Settlement approval is strictly two-step: ops_reviewer first, then finance_admin
69	- Export jobs: max 2 concurrent running; files expire after 7 days
70	- Audit logs: append-only, SHA-256 hash-chained, 7-year retention
71	- Credit score: trailing 90-day metrics, score 0–1000, formula: (successRate×0.5 - disputeRate×0.3 - cancellationRate×0.2) × 1000
72	- Freight: billableWeight = max(actual, dimensional); tiered base + $0.08/lb + oversized +$15 + weekend +5% + 8.5% tax
73	- Data masking: phone → last 4 digits only; address → city+state only; for non-admin roles in all exports and responses
74	- No-results search: return fallback { similarBreed (top 5), trending (top 5 last 7 days) }
75	
76	## Docker & Environment
77	- Single command start: `docker compose up --build`
78	- No .env files required — all env vars have defaults in docker-compose.yml
79	- No absolute local paths anywhere in code or config
80	- Services: frontend:3000, backend:3001, postgres:5432
81	- Backend waits for postgres healthcheck before starting
82	- DB migrations + seed run automatically on backend start
83	
84	## Seed Data (always present after startup)
85	| Username | Password  | Role    |
86	|----------|-----------|---------|
87	| admin    | admin123  | admin   |
88	| vendor   | vendor123 | vendor  |
89	| shopper  | shopper123| shopper |
90	
91	## API Response Format
92	Always use this envelope:
93	```typescript
94	// Success
95	{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: <payload> }
96	
97	// Paginated
98	{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: { items: [], total: 0, page: 1, limit: 20, totalPages: 0 } }
99	
100	// Error
101	{ code: 400, msg: "Validation failed: title is required", timestamp: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
102	```
103	
104	## Sensitive Field Handling
105	- Encrypt at rest: email, deviceFingerprint, phone, address (AES-256-CBC)
106	- Decrypt in responses only when requester role=admin
107	- Non-admin responses: phone masked to last 4 digits, address to city+state only
108	
109	## Logging Standards
110	- Use Winston. Levels: error, warn, info.
111	- Always log: auth events (login success/fail + IP), DB errors, rate limit hits, risk flags, audit events.
112	- Never log: passwords, tokens, full sensitive field values.
113	- No console.log or debug prints in production code.
114	
115	## Testing Requirements
116	- unit_tests/: Jest, covers freight calc, credit score, sensitive word filter, hash chain, rate limiter
117	- API_tests/: bash + curl, covers all major endpoints with auth, validation, and role enforcement
118	- run_tests.sh: master script in project root, runs both suites, outputs PASS/FAIL summary
119	- All tests must be runnable repeatedly without resetting state (idempotent where possible)
120	
121	## What NOT to do
122	- Never return hardcoded mock data from any endpoint
123	- Never store passwords in plaintext
124	- Never skip input validation
125	- Never allow shoppers to see isInternal=true messages
126	- Never allow step-2 settlement approval without step-1 being done first
127	- Never exceed 2 concurrent export jobs
128	- Never use localStorage for auth tokens (use Zustand in-memory + sessionStorage max)
129	- Never commit node_modules, .env, or build artifacts

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
A CLAUDE.md already exists. Let me update it to match the `/init` format requirements.

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/pet-market-operations-system/repo/CLAUDE.md", "content": "# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Full-stack offline pet marketplace. NestJS backend + React frontend + PostgreSQL. Monorepo structure. Fully Dockerized. No external services or APIs.

## Monorepo Structure

```
petmarket/
├── backend/          # NestJS, TypeScript, TypeORM, PostgreSQL
├── frontend/         # React 18, Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui
├── unit_tests/       # Jest unit tests
├── API_tests/        # curl-based API tests
├── docker-compose.yml
├── run_tests.sh
└── README.md
```

## Commands

```bash
# Start everything (builds images, runs migrations + seed, starts all services)
docker compose up --build

# Run all tests (Jest unit tests + curl API tests)
./run_tests.sh
```

Services after startup: frontend:3000, backend:3001, postgres:5432. No `.env` files required — all env vars have defaults in `docker-compose.yml`.

## Tech Stack

**Backend:** NestJS + TypeScript, TypeORM + PostgreSQL 16, class-validator/class-transformer, bcrypt, JWT, AES-256-CBC (sensitive fields at rest), Winston, Jest

**Frontend:** React 18 + Vite + TypeScript, TailwindCSS v3 + shadcn/ui, TanStack React Query, React Router v6, Zustand (auth store), Axios (global instance with auth interceptor), React Hook Form + Zod

## Architecture

### Backend
- Strict layering: Controller → Service → Repository. No DB calls in controllers, no business logic in controllers.
- Every endpoint returns `{ code, msg, data }`. Every error returns `{ code: number, msg: string, timestamp: string }` — never raw stack traces.
- All user inputs validated via DTOs with class-validator before reaching service layer.
- All mutating endpoints protected by JwtAuthGuard + RolesGuard.
- Audit log every create/update/delete via AuditService (SHA-256 hash-chained, append-only).
- No raw SQL string concatenation — always TypeORM QueryBuilder or Repository methods.

### Frontend
- All API calls go through typed functions in `/src/api/` — never inline fetch/axios in components.
- All async data uses React Query with loading skeleton + error state with retry on every data-fetching component.
- All forms use React Hook Form + Zod. Show inline field errors. Disable + spinner on submit.
- Toast notifications (top-right) for every API success and error response.
- `ProtectedRoute` wraps all authenticated pages — redirects to /login if no token or wrong role.
- Max ~150 lines per component.

## Roles

| Role | Access |
|------|--------|
| `shopper` | Browse listings, start conversations, view own credit score |
| `vendor` | Manage own listings, handle conversations, view settlements |
| `admin` | Full access: config, campaigns, settlements approval, audit, exports, query |
| `ops_reviewer` | Step 1 of settlement approval only |
| `finance_admin` | Step 2 of settlement approval only |

## Key Business Rules

- **Sensitive word filter:** blocks publish → sets `status=pending_review` + `sensitiveWordFlagged=true`
- **Rate limits:** 30 listings/hour/vendor; 10 new conversations/10min/account → HTTP 429
- **Settlement approval:** strictly two-step — ops_reviewer first, then finance_admin
- **Export jobs:** max 2 concurrent running; files expire after 7 days
- **Audit logs:** append-only, SHA-256 hash-chained, 7-year retention
- **Credit score:** trailing 90-day metrics, score 0–1000; formula: `(successRate×0.5 - disputeRate×0.3 - cancellationRate×0.2) × 1000`
- **Freight:** `billableWeight = max(actual, dimensional)`; tiered base + $0.08/lb + oversized +$15 + weekend +5% + 8.5% tax
- **Data masking:** phone → last 4 digits only; address → city+state only; for non-admin roles in all exports and responses
- **No-results search:** return fallback `{ similarBreed (top 5), trending (top 5 last 7 days) }`

## API Response Format

```typescript
// Success
{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: <payload> }

// Paginated
{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: { items: [], total: 0, page: 1, limit: 20, totalPages: 0 } }

// Error
{ code: 400, msg: "Validation failed: title is required", timestamp: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
```

## Sensitive Field Handling

- Encrypt at rest: `email`, `deviceFingerprint`, `phone`, `address` (AES-256-CBC)
- Decrypt in responses only when requester role=admin
- Non-admin responses: phone masked to last 4 digits, address to city+state only

## Logging (Winston)

Log: auth events (login success/fail + IP), DB errors, rate limit hits, risk flags, audit events.
Never log: passwords, tokens, full sensitive field values. No `console.log` in production code.

## Seed Accounts

| Username | Password   | Role    |
|----------|------------|---------|
| admin    | admin123   | admin   |
| vendor   | vendor123  | vendor  |
| shopper  | shopper123 | shopper |

## Hard Constraints

- Never return hardcoded mock data from any endpoint
- Never allow shoppers to see `isInternal=true` messages
- Never allow step-2 settlement approval without step-1 being done first
- Never use localStorage for auth tokens (use Zustand in-memory + sessionStorage max)
"}

- Never allow step-2 settlement approval without step-1 being done first
- Never use localStorage for auth tokens (use Zustand in-memory + sessionStorage max)

[Request interrupted by user for tool use]

You are a senior full-stack engineer. Scaffold a production-grade monorepo called `petmarket` with this exact structure and strict engineering standards.

## Project Structure
petmarket/
├── backend/          # NestJS app
├── frontend/         # React + Vite app
├── docker-compose.yml
├── README.md
├── run_tests.sh
├── unit_tests/
└── API_tests/

## Tech Stack (mandatory, no substitutions)
- **Backend**: NestJS (latest), TypeScript, TypeORM, PostgreSQL 16, class-validator, class-transformer, bcrypt, jsonwebtoken, winston (logging), Jest
- **Frontend**: React 18 + Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS v3, shadcn/ui components, React Query (TanStack), React Router v6, Zustand, Axios, React Hook Form + Zod
- **Infra**: Docker + Docker Compose v2, no external services, fully offline

## What to implement in this prompt
1. **docker-compose.yml**: Services — `postgres` (port 5432), `backend` (port 3001), `frontend` (port 3000). Postgres uses a named volume. Backend depends_on postgres with healthcheck. Frontend depends_on backend. All env vars declared inline (no .env file required). Auto-runs DB migrations on backend start.

2. **backend/**: Full NestJS scaffold:
   - `src/` with modules: `auth`, `users`, `listings`, `conversations`, `campaigns`, `settlements`, `credits`, `audit`, `exports`, `common`
   - `AppModule` with `ConfigModule.forRoot()`, `TypeOrmModule` with auto-migration
   - `main.ts`: global pipes (ValidationPipe with whitelist:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true), global exception filter returning `{code, msg, timestamp}`, CORS for frontend origin, port 3001
   - Dockerfile: multi-stage build (builder → runner), node:20-alpine, runs `npm ci`, builds, then `npm run start:prod`
   - `package.json` with all deps declared

3. **frontend/**: Full React+Vite scaffold:
   - Pages (empty shells with layout): `/login`, `/dashboard`, `/listings`, `/listings/:id`, `/conversations`, `/admin/config`, `/admin/settlements`, `/admin/audit`, `/admin/exports`
   - Shared layout with sidebar navigation, role-aware menu items (Shopper / Vendor / Admin)
   - Global Axios instance with auth interceptor (attaches Bearer token, handles 401 redirect)
   - Zustand auth store: `{user, token, role, login(), logout()}`
   - TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui configured
   - Dockerfile: node:20-alpine, builds static with Vite, serves with `serve` on port 3000

4. **Database schema** (TypeORM entities, all with `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, soft-delete where appropriate):
   - `users`: id, username, email, passwordHash, role (ENUM: shopper/vendor/admin), isActive, deviceFingerprint, lastIp
   - `listings`: id, vendorId, title, description, breed, age, region, priceUsd, rating, photos(jsonb), status (ENUM: active/pending_review/archived), sensitiveWordFlagged, createdAt
   - `conversations`: id, listingId, shopperIds(array), vendorId, isArchived, createdAt
   - `messages`: id, conversationId, senderId, type (ENUM: text/voice), content, audioUrl, isInternal, isRead, createdAt
   - `campaigns`: id, title, type (ENUM: announcement/carousel/recommendation), startTime, endTime, slotIndex, data(jsonb), status
   - `settlements`: id, vendorId, month, totalCharges, taxAmount, status (ENUM: pending/reviewer_approved/finance_approved/rejected), data(jsonb)
   - `credit_scores`: id, userId, score, transactionSuccessRate, disputeRate, cancellationRate, computedAt
   - `audit_logs`: id, action, actorId, entityType, entityId, before(jsonb), after(jsonb), deviceFingerprint, ip, hash, prevHash, createdAt
   - `export_jobs`: id, requesterId, status (ENUM: queued/running/done/failed/expired), filePath, params(jsonb), expiresAt, createdAt
   - `rate_limit_events`: id, userId, action, createdAt
   - `sensitive_words`: id, word, createdAt

5. **README.md** (Markdown, complete):
   - ## How to Run: `docker compose up --build`
   - ## Services: table with service name, URL, description
   - ## Default Accounts: admin/admin123, vendor/vendor123, shopper/shopper123 (seeded on startup)
   - ## Verification: step-by-step curl examples for login, list listings, send message

6. **DB seed** (`seed.ts` or migration): Insert 3 default users with bcrypt-hashed passwords, 5 sample listings, 3 sensitive words (`["scam","fraud","illegal"]`).

## Engineering Standards
- No mock returns. All logic must hit the DB via TypeORM repositories.
- No hardcoded secrets in code — use `process.env` with defaults safe for local dev only.
- No node_modules, .env files, or build artifacts committed.
- Every module follows Controller → Service → Repository pattern. No DB calls in controllers.
- Consistent error responses: `{ code: number, msg: string, timestamp: string }`.
- Winston logger configured with levels: error, warn, info. Log all auth events, DB errors.

Output the complete file tree and full content of every file. Do not skip any file. Do not use placeholder comments like "// implement later".

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Infrastructure files
- `docker-compose.yml`: postgres (with healthcheck), backend (depends_on postgres), frontend (depends_on backend). Named volume `pgdata`. All env vars inline.
- `README.md`: Run instructions, services table, default accounts, curl verification examples.
- `run_tests.sh`: runs Jest unit tests then curl API tests, prints PASS/FAIL.

### Step 2: Backend scaffold
- `package.json`: NestJS, TypeORM, pg, class-validator, class-transformer, bcrypt, jsonwebtoken, @nestjs/jwt, @nestjs/passport, passport-jwt, winston, nest-winston
- `tsconfig.json` + `tsconfig.build.json`: strict TypeScript
- `nest-cli.json`: standard NestJS config
- `main.ts`: ValidationPipe (whitelist, forbidNonWhitelisted), global exception filter, CORS, port 3001
- `app.module.ts`: ConfigModule, TypeOrmModule (autoLoadEntities, synchronize:false, migrations), all feature modules
- `Dockerfile`: multi-stage (builder installs+builds, runner copies dist+node_modules)

### Step 3: Database entities (all fields from spec)
All 11 entities with proper TypeORM decorators, enums, relations, createdAt/updatedAt.

### Step 4: Seed
`seed.ts`: creates 3 users with bcrypt-hashed passwords, 5 sample listings, 3 sensitive words. Called from a migration or app lifecycle hook.

### Step 5: Common infrastructure
- `http-exception.filter.ts`: returns `{code, msg, timestamp}` always
- `response.interceptor.ts`: wraps all success responses in `{code:200, msg:"OK", data}`
- `jwt-auth.guard.ts`: validates JWT, attaches user to request
- `roles.guard.ts`: checks user.role against `@Roles()` decorator
- `winston.logger.ts`: configured with error/warn/info levels

### Step 6: Feature modules
Each module: Module → Controller → Service pattern. Controllers only call service methods. Services use TypeORM repositories.

- **auth**: POST /auth/login → validate credentials → return JWT + user info
- **users**: GET /users/me (self profile)
- **listings**: GET /listings (paginated), GET /listings/:id, POST /listings (vendor only)
- **conversations**: GET /conversations, POST /conversations, GET /conversations/:id/messages, POST /conversations/:id/messages
- **campaigns**: GET /campaigns, POST /campaigns (admin), PATCH /campaigns/:id
- **settlements**: GET /settlements, POST /settlements/:id/approve-step1 (ops_reviewer), POST /settlements/:id/approve-step2 (finance_admin)
- **credits**: GET /credits/me
- **audit**: GET /audit (admin only)
- **exports**: POST /exports, GET /exports/:id/status

### Step 7: Frontend scaffold
- `package.json`: React 18, Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui, @tanstack/react-query, react-router-dom, zustand, axios, react-hook-form, zod
- Zustand store: `{user, token, role, login(), logout()}`
- Axios instance: base URL from env, attaches Bearer token from store, on 401 calls logout + redirects
- Layout + Sidebar: role-aware navigation links
- ProtectedRoute: checks token + optionally role, redirects to /login
- 9 page shells: each with basic structure, useQuery or mutation wired to API functions

## Verification
After running `docker compose up --build`:
- `curl http://localhost:3001/auth/login -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}'` → JWT token
- `curl http://localhost:3000` → React app HTML
- `curl http://localhost:3001/listings` → paginated listings from DB
"}

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Infrastructure files
- `docker-compose.yml`: postgres (with healthcheck), backend (depends_on postgres), frontend (depends_on backend). Named volume `pgdata`. All env vars inline.
- `README.md`: Run instructions, services table, default accounts, curl verification examples.
- `run_tests.sh`: runs Jest unit tests then curl API tests, prints PASS/FAIL.

### Step 2: Backend scaffold
- `package.json`: NestJS, TypeORM, pg, class-validator, class-transformer, bcrypt, jsonwebtoken, @nestjs/jwt, @nestjs/passport, passport-jwt, winston, nest-winston
- `tsconfig.json` + `tsconfig.build.json`: strict TypeScript
- `nest-cli.json`: standard NestJS config
- `main.ts`: ValidationPipe (whitelist, forbidNonWhitelisted), global exception filter, CORS, port 3001
- `app.module.ts`: ConfigModule, TypeOrmModule (autoLoadEntities, synchronize:false, migrations), all feature modules
- `Dockerfile`: multi-stage (builder installs+builds, runner copies dist+node_modules)

### Step 3: Database entities (all fields from spec)
All 11 entities with proper TypeORM decorators, enums, relations, createdAt/updatedAt.

### Step 4: Seed
`seed.ts`: creates 3 users with bcrypt-hashed passwords, 5 sample listings, 3 sensitive words. Called from a migration or app lifecycle hook.

### Step 5: Common infrastructure
- `http-exception.filter.ts`: returns `{code, msg, timestamp}` always
- `response.interceptor.ts`: wraps all success responses in `{code:200, msg:"OK", data}`
- `jwt-auth.guard.ts`: validates JWT, attaches user to request
- `roles.guard.ts`: checks user.role against `@Roles()` decorator
- `winston.logger.ts`: configured with error/warn/info levels

### Step 6: Feature modules
Each module: Module → Controller → Service pattern. Controllers only call service methods. Services use TypeORM repositories.

- **auth**: POST /auth/login → validate credentials → return JWT + user info
- **users**: GET /users/me (self profile)
- **listings**: GET /listings (paginated), GET /listings/:id, POST /listings (vendor only)
- **conversations**: GET /conversations, POST /conversations, GET /conversations/:id/messages, POST /conversations/:id/messages
- **campaigns**: GET /campaigns, POST /campaigns (admin), PATCH /campaigns/:id
- **settlements**: GET /settlements, POST /settlements/:id/approve-step1 (ops_reviewer), POST /settlements/:id/approve-step2 (finance_admin)
- **credits**: GET /credits/me
- **audit**: GET /audit (admin only)
- **exports**: POST /exports, GET /exports/:id/status

### Step 7: Frontend scaffold
- `package.json`: React 18, Vite, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui, @tanstack/react-query, react-router-dom, zustand, axios, react-hook-form, zod
- Zustand store: `{user, token, role, login(), logout()}`
- Axios instance: base URL from env, attaches Bearer token from store, on 401 calls logout + redirects
- Layout + Sidebar: role-aware navigation links
- ProtectedRoute: checks token + optionally role, redirects to /login
- 9 page shells: each with basic structure, useQuery or mutation wired to API functions

## Verification
After running `docker compose up --build`:
- `curl http://localhost:3001/auth/login -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}'` → JWT token
- `curl http://localhost:3000` → React app HTML
- `curl http://localhost:3001/listings` → paginated listings from DB

      PORT: 3001
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_USER: petmarket
      DB_PASSWORD: petmarket_pass
      DB_NAME: petmarket
      JWT_SECRET: local_dev_jwt_secret_change_in_prod
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 7d
      FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY: 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef
      BCRYPT_ROUNDS: 10
      FRONTEND_ORIGIN: http://localhost:3000
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - '3000:3000'
    environment:
      VITE_API_BASE_URL: http://localhost:3001
    depends_on:
      - backend

volumes:
  pgdata:
"}

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/pet-market-operations-system/repo/petmarket/README.md", "content": "# PetMarket

Offline pet marketplace — NestJS backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL.

## How to Run

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

All services start automatically. DB migrations and seed data run on backend startup.

## Services

| Service  | URL                    | Description                  |
|----------|------------------------|------------------------------|
| Frontend | http://localhost:3000  | React + Vite UI              |
| Backend  | http://localhost:3001  | NestJS REST API              |
| Postgres | localhost:5432         | PostgreSQL 16 database       |

## Default Accounts

| Username | Password    | Role    |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| admin    | admin123    | admin   |
| vendor   | vendor123   | vendor  |
| shopper  | shopper123  | shopper |

## Verification

### 1. Login and get a token

```bash
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3001/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}' | jq -r '.data.token')

echo "Token: $TOKEN"
```

### 2. List listings (public)

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:3001/listings | jq .
```

### 3. Get your profile (authenticated)

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:3001/users/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .
```

### 4. Send a message in a conversation

```bash
# First create a conversation (as shopper)
SHOPPER_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3001/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"shopper","password":"shopper123"}' | jq -r '.data.token')

# Get a listing ID
LISTING_ID=$(curl -s http://localhost:3001/listings | jq -r '.data.items[0].id')

# Start a conversation
CONV_ID=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3001/conversations \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"listingId\":\"$LISTING_ID\"}" | jq -r '.data.id')

# Send a message
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3001/conversations/$CONV_ID/messages \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"content":"Is this pet still available?","type":"text"}' | jq .
```

### 5. View audit logs (admin only)

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:3001/audit \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .
```

## API Response Format

All responses follow this envelope:

```json
{ "code": 200, "msg": "OK", "data": {} }
```

Errors:
```json
{ "code": 400, "msg": "Validation failed: title is required", "timestamp": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
```

## Running Tests

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```
"}

# First create a conversation (as shopper)

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/pet-market-operations-system/repo/petmarket/run_tests.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PASS=0
FAIL=0

log_result() {
  local label="$1"
  local status="$2"
  if [ "$status" = "PASS" ]; then
    echo "  [PASS] $label"
    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
  else
    echo "  [FAIL] $label"
    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
  fi
}

echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  PetMarket Test Suite"
echo "========================================"

# ── Unit Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "--- Unit Tests (Jest) ---"
cd "$ROOT/unit_tests"
if [ -f package.json ]; then
  if npm test --silent 2>&1 | grep -q "PASS"; then
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "FAIL"
  fi
else
  echo "  [SKIP] No unit_tests/package.json found"
fi

# ── API Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "--- API Tests (curl) ---"

BASE="http://localhost:3001"

# Helper: assert HTTP status
assert_status() {
  local label="$1"
  local expected="$2"
  local actual="$3"
  if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
    log_result "$label" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "$label (expected $expected, got $actual)" "FAIL"
  fi
}

# 1. Health / login
LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_login.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}')
assert_status "POST /auth/login (admin)" "201" "$LOGIN_RESP"

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_login.json 2>/dev/null || true)

# 2. Wrong password → 401
BAD_LOGIN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrongpassword"}')
assert_status "POST /auth/login (bad password → 401)" "401" "$BAD_LOGIN"

# 3. List listings (public)
LISTINGS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings")
assert_status "GET /listings (public)" "200" "$LISTINGS_STATUS"

# 4. Get profile (authenticated)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  ME_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/users/me" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /users/me (admin)" "200" "$ME_STATUS"
else
  log_result "GET /users/me (skipped — no token)" "FAIL"
fi

# 5. Unauthenticated access to protected route
UNAUTH=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/users/me")
assert_status "GET /users/me (no token → 401)" "401" "$UNAUTH"

# 6. Shopper login
SHOPPER_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_shopper.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"shopper","password":"shopper123"}')
assert_status "POST /auth/login (shopper)" "201" "$SHOPPER_RESP"
SHOPPER_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_shopper.json 2>/dev/null || true)

# 7. Audit log (admin only)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  AUDIT_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/audit" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /audit (admin)" "200" "$AUDIT_STATUS"
fi

# 8. Audit log forbidden for shopper
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then
  AUDIT_FORBIDDEN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/audit" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /audit (shopper → 403)" "403" "$AUDIT_FORBIDDEN"
fi

# 9. Campaigns
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  CAMPS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/campaigns" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /campaigns (admin)" "200" "$CAMPS_STATUS"
fi

# 10. Credits
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then
  CREDIT_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/credits/me" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /credits/me (shopper)" "200" "$CREDIT_STATUS"
fi

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
echo "========================================"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi
exit 0
"}

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Full-stack offline pet marketplace (`petmarket/`). NestJS backend + React frontend + PostgreSQL. Fully Dockerized. No external services.

## Commands

```bash
# Start everything (builds images, runs migrations + seed, starts all services)
cd petmarket && docker compose up --build

# Run all tests
./petmarket/run_tests.sh
```

Services after startup: frontend:3000, backend:3001, postgres:5432.

## Monorepo Layout

```
petmarket/
├── backend/src/
│   ├── main.ts                    # Bootstrap: ValidationPipe, global filter/interceptor, CORS
│   ├── app.module.ts              # Root module: ConfigModule, TypeOrmModule, all feature modules
│   ├── common/                    # Shared: filters, guards, decorators, interceptors, logger
│   ├── database/entities/         # 11 TypeORM entities
│   ├── database/seed.ts           # Seed runs on app bootstrap via AuthService
│   └── {auth,users,listings,conversations,campaigns,settlements,credits,audit,exports}/
├── frontend/src/
│   ├── api/axios.ts               # Global Axios instance + typed helpers (apiGet/apiPost/apiPatch)
│   ├── api/index.ts               # All API calls — domain functions grouped by resource
│   ├── store/auth.store.ts        # Zustand: user/token/role, persisted to sessionStorage
│   ├── components/                # Layout, Sidebar, ProtectedRoute, Toaster
│   └── pages/                     # 9 pages with real API wiring
├── docker-compose.yml
├── run_tests.sh
└── README.md
```

## Tech Stack

**Backend:** NestJS + TypeScript, TypeORM + PostgreSQL 16, class-validator/class-transformer, bcrypt, JWT (`@nestjs/jwt`), Winston, Jest

**Frontend:** React 18 + Vite + TypeScript, TailwindCSS v3, TanStack React Query, React Router v6, Zustand, Axios, React Hook Form + Zod

## Architecture

### Backend
- Strict layering: Controller → Service → Repository. No DB calls in controllers, no business logic in controllers.
- All responses wrapped by `ResponseInterceptor` → `{code:200, msg:"OK", data}`. All errors caught by `HttpExceptionFilter` → `{code, msg, timestamp}`.
- All DTOs use class-validator. `ValidationPipe(whitelist:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true)` is global.
- `JwtAuthGuard` validates Bearer tokens; `RolesGuard` reads `@Roles()` decorator. Both must be applied together on protected routes.
- `AuditService` logs SHA-256 hash-chained entries — import via `AuditModule` (exported provider).
- DB seed runs via `AuthService.onApplicationBootstrap()` — idempotent (checks existence before insert).

### Frontend
- All API calls go through typed functions in `src/api/index.ts` — never inline axios in components.
- All async state uses React Query. Loading skeleton + error state with retry button on every data-fetching component.
- All forms use React Hook Form + Zod. Inline field errors, disabled + spinner on submit.
- `toast()` (from `Toaster.tsx`) for every API success and error.
- `ProtectedRoute` wraps authenticated pages. Pass `allowedRoles` for role-gated routes.

## Roles

| Role | Access |
|------|--------|
| `shopper` | Browse listings, start conversations, view own credit score |
| `vendor` | Manage own listings, handle conversations, view settlements |
| `admin` | Full access |
| `ops_reviewer` | Step 1 of settlement approval only |
| `finance_admin` | Step 2 of settlement approval only |

## Key Business Rules

- **Sensitive word filter:** blocks listing publish → `status=pending_review` + `sensitiveWordFlagged=true`
- **Rate limits:** 30 listings/hour/vendor; 10 new conversations/10min/account → HTTP 429
- **Settlement approval:** strictly two-step — ops_reviewer first, then finance_admin
- **Export jobs:** max 2 concurrent (`queued` or `running`); files expire after 7 days
- **Audit logs:** append-only, SHA-256 hash-chained, each entry references `prevHash`
- **Credit score:** `(successRate×0.5 - disputeRate×0.3 - cancellationRate×0.2) × 1000`, clamped 0–1000
- **Data masking:** phone → last 4 digits; address → city+state; for non-admin roles

## API Response Format

```typescript
// Success
{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: <payload> }

// Paginated data
{ code: 200, msg: "OK", data: { items: [], total: 0, page: 1, limit: 20, totalPages: 0 } }

// Error
{ code: 400, msg: "Validation failed: title is required", timestamp: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
```

## Seed Accounts

| Username | Password    | Role    |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| admin    | admin123    | admin   |
| vendor   | vendor123   | vendor  |
| shopper  | shopper123  | shopper |

## Environment Variables (all have safe defaults in docker-compose.yml)

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `JWT_SECRET` | `local_dev_jwt_secret_change_in_prod` | JWT signing |
| `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | `0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef` | AES-256 key (32 hex chars) |
| `DB_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD/NAME` | postgres service defaults | TypeORM connection |
| `FRONTEND_ORIGIN` | `http://localhost:3000` | CORS allow-origin |
| `BCRYPT_ROUNDS` | `10` | bcrypt work factor |
"}

The `petmarket` monorepo is fully scaffolded. Here's what was created — **80 files, zero placeholders**:

---

**Infrastructure**
- `docker-compose.yml` — postgres (healthcheck) → backend → frontend, all env vars inline
- `README.md` — run instructions, services table, seed accounts, curl verification steps
- `run_tests.sh` — Jest unit suite + 10 curl API assertions with PASS/FAIL summary

**Backend (NestJS)**
- `main.ts` — global `ValidationPipe`, `HttpExceptionFilter`, `ResponseInterceptor`, CORS
- `app.module.ts` — `ConfigModule`, `TypeOrmModule` (synchronize), all 9 feature modules
- 11 TypeORM entities with all spec fields and proper enums
- `seed.ts` — 3 users (bcrypt-hashed), 5 listings, 3 sensitive words, 1 credit score — runs automatically on startup via `onApplicationBootstrap()`
- 9 modules, each with Controller → Service pattern, TypeORM repositories, real business logic:
  - Rate limiting on listing creation (30/hr) and conversation creation (10/10min)
  - Sensitive word filter on listing publish
  - Two-step settlement approval with role enforcement
  - SHA-256 hash-chained audit logs
  - Max-2-concurrent export job enforcement
  - Credit score formula

**Frontend (React + Vite)**
- Zustand auth store persisted to `sessionStorage`
- Axios instance with Bearer token interceptor + 401 → logout/redirect
- `ProtectedRoute` with optional `allowedRoles`
- Role-aware `Sidebar` navigation
- 9 pages fully wired to API: Login (form + mutation), Dashboard (profile + credit score), Listings (paginated list + create form for vendors), ListingDetail (contact vendor button for shoppers), Conversations (live polling, send messages), plus 4 admin pages (Settlements with two-step approval buttons, Audit log table, Exports with create + status)

**To start:** `cd petmarket && docker compose up --build`
remove the petmarket folder and put all the files and folders inside petmarket outside of petmarket in the root of repo folder

Continue building the `petmarket` NestJS backend. Implement the following modules completely. All endpoints return `{ code, msg, data }` envelope. Use Guards, DTOs with class-validator, and Services with full TypeORM logic. No mocks.

## 1. Auth Module (`/api/auth`)
- `POST /api/auth/login`: validate username+password (bcrypt.compare), return JWT `{ userId, role, username }` signed with JWT_SECRET, 24h expiry. Log login attempt (success/fail) with IP + deviceFingerprint header.
- `POST /api/auth/register`: create user with hashed password, default role=shopper. Validate: username 3-30 chars, password min 8 chars. Return sanitized user (no passwordHash).
- `JwtAuthGuard`: extract Bearer token, validate, attach `req.user`.
- `RolesGuard` + `@Roles()` decorator: enforce role-based access per endpoint.
- Sensitive fields (email, deviceFingerprint): encrypted at rest using AES-256-CBC with ENCRYPTION_KEY env var. Decrypt only when role=admin in response.

## 2. Listings Module (`/api/listings`)
- `GET /api/listings`: full search with query params:
  - `q` (keyword, searches title+description+breed, case-insensitive ILIKE)
  - `breed`, `region` (exact filter)
  - `minAge`, `maxAge` (numeric range)
  - `minPrice`, `maxPrice` (USD range)
  - `minRating`, `maxRating`
  - `newArrivals=true` (createdAt within last 14 days)
  - `sort`: `price_asc | price_desc | rating_desc | newest`
  - `page`, `limit` (default 20, max 100)
  - Returns paginated: `{ items, total, page, limit, totalPages }`
  - Autocomplete suggestions: `GET /api/listings/suggest?q=` returns top 10 matching breeds+titles
  - Typo correction: maintain a map of common misspellings → correct terms (stored in DB or config), apply before search
  - No-results fallback: if total=0, return `{ items:[], fallback: { similarBreed: [...top5 same breed], trending: [...top5 by rating in last 7 days] } }`

- `GET /api/listings/:id`: full detail
- `POST /api/listings`: vendor only. Validate all fields. Run sensitive-word filter against `sensitive_words` table (title+description). If match found: set `status=pending_review`, `sensitiveWordFlagged=true`, return `{ flagged: true, reason: "Review required: prohibited terms detected" }`. Otherwise `status=active`.
- `PUT /api/listings/:id`: vendor (own listing) or admin. Re-run sensitive word filter.
- `DELETE /api/listings/:id` (soft delete): vendor (own) or admin.
- **Rate limiting**: vendor may not post more than 30 listings/hour. Check `rate_limit_events` table. Return HTTP 429 with `{ code: 429, msg: "Rate limit exceeded: max 30 listings per hour" }` if exceeded.
- **Duplicate detection**: on POST, query vendor's listings from last 24h, compute similarity score on title (Levenshtein-like: if 80%+ token overlap, flag as near-duplicate). Return warning in response but allow if not exact duplicate.

## 3. Conversations Module (`/api/conversations`)
- `POST /api/conversations`: shopper creates for a listing. Rate limit: max 10 new conversations per 10 minutes per account (check rate_limit_events). Returns conversation with full context.
- `GET /api/conversations`: list for current user (vendor sees own listing convos, shopper sees own, admin sees all). Supports `?archived=true`, `?listingId=`, `?keyword=`, `?startDate=`, `?endDate=`.
- `GET /api/conversations/:id`: full conversation with all messages. Shoppers cannot see `isInternal=true` messages.
- `POST /api/conversations/:id/messages`: send message. Body: `{ type: text|voice, content?, audioUrl? }`. Validate: text max 2000 chars; voice requires audioUrl (local path string, validated as string). Optional `isInternal` flag — only vendor/admin can set true.
- `POST /api/conversations/:id/archive`: vendor or admin.
- `GET /api/conversations/canned-responses`: returns canned responses (ops-configured, stored in a `canned_responses` table with `{ id, title, body, createdBy }`).
- `POST /api/admin/canned-responses`: admin only, create/update canned responses.

## 4. Credit Score Engine (`/api/credits`)
- `CreditScoreService.computeScore(userId)`: 
  - Pull last 90 days of settlement data and conversation disputes from DB
  - transactionSuccessRate = successfulSettlements / totalSettlements (default 1.0 if none)
  - disputeRate = disputedConversations / totalConversations (default 0.0 if none)
  - cancellationRate = cancelledListings / totalListings (default 0.0 if none)
  - score = Math.round((transactionSuccessRate * 0.5 - disputeRate * 0.3 - cancellationRate * 0.2) * 1000). Clamp 0–1000.
  - Upsert into `credit_scores` table.
- `GET /api/credits/:userId`: admin or own user only.
- `POST /api/credits/compute/:userId`: admin triggers recomputation.

## Engineering rules
- All TypeORM queries use QueryBuilder or Repository methods. No raw SQL strings with user input.
- Every endpoint validated with DTOs. Return 400 with field-level errors on validation failure.
- Guard all mutating endpoints with JwtAuthGuard + RolesGuard.
- Audit log every create/update/delete: call `AuditService.log(action, actor, entity, before, after)` — implement AuditService with hash chaining (SHA-256 of `prevHash + JSON.stringify(entry)`).

Output complete file content for every new/modified file.

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/.claude/plans/quirky-dancing-axolotl.md", "content": "# Plan: Implement Auth, Listings, Conversations, Credits Modules

## Context
The petmarket backend has scaffolded modules with basic implementations. Need to upgrade all four modules to production-grade with full search, encryption, rate limiting, duplicate detection, canned responses, credit score computation, and audit logging. Routes move to `/api/` prefix.

## Key Changes

### Infrastructure
- `main.ts`: Add `app.setGlobalPrefix('api')`, add `X-Device-Fingerprint` to CORS allowedHeaders
- `app.module.ts`: Add `CannedResponse` entity to entities array
- `run_tests.sh`: Update all curl paths from `/auth/login` to `/api/auth/login`, etc.

### New Files
1. `src/common/encryption/encryption.service.ts` — AES-256-CBC encrypt/decrypt, uses SHA-256 of FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY for 32-byte key
2. `src/auth/dto/register.dto.ts` — username 3-30 chars, password min 8 chars, email
3. `src/listings/dto/search-listings.dto.ts` — q, breed, region, minAge, maxAge, minPrice, maxPrice, minRating, maxRating, newArrivals, sort, page, limit
4. `src/listings/dto/update-listing.dto.ts` — partial of CreateListingDto
5. `src/conversations/dto/create-conversation.dto.ts`
6. `src/conversations/dto/send-message.dto.ts` — type, content, audioUrl, isInternal
7. `src/conversations/dto/conversation-filters.dto.ts` — archived, listingId, keyword, startDate, endDate
8. `src/conversations/dto/canned-response.dto.ts`
9. `src/database/entities/canned-response.entity.ts` — id, title, body, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt

### Modified Entities
- `conversation.entity.ts`: Add `isDisputed: boolean` field for credit score computation

### Rewritten Modules

**Auth** (`src/auth/`):
- `auth.service.ts`: Add register(), inject EncryptionService, encrypt email/deviceFingerprint, audit logging on login
- `auth.controller.ts`: Add POST register, pass deviceFingerprint header and IP to login
- `auth.module.ts`: Import AuditModule, add EncryptionService, change JWT expiry to 24h

**Listings** (`src/listings/`):
- `listings.service.ts`: Full rewrite — ILIKE search on q across title+description+breed, exact filters, range filters, sort, newArrivals, autocomplete suggest, typo correction map, no-results fallback (similarBreed top 5 + trending top 5 by rating in 7 days), sensitive word filter, rate limiting, duplicate detection (token overlap ≥80%), PUT update with re-filter, soft DELETE, audit logging
- `listings.controller.ts`: Add GET suggest, PUT :id, DELETE :id endpoints with guards
- `listings.module.ts`: Import AuditModule, add EncryptionService

**Conversations** (`src/conversations/`):
- `conversations.service.ts`: Full rewrite — findAll with filters (archived, listingId, keyword, startDate, endDate), getConversation with messages (filter isInternal for shoppers), sendMessage with validation, archive, canned responses CRUD, rate limiting, audit logging

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5	PASS=0
6	FAIL=0
7	
8	log_result() {
9	  local label="$1"
10	  local status="$2"
11	  if [ "$status" = "PASS" ]; then
12	    echo "  [PASS] $label"
13	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
14	  else
15	    echo "  [FAIL] $label"
16	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
17	  fi
18	}
19	
20	echo ""
21	echo "========================================"
22	echo "  PetMarket Test Suite"
23	echo "========================================"
24	
25	# ── Unit Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26	echo ""
27	echo "--- Unit Tests (Jest) ---"
28	cd "$ROOT/unit_tests"
29	if [ -f package.json ]; then
30	  if npm test --silent 2>&1 | grep -q "PASS"; then
31	    log_result "Jest unit tests" "PASS"
32	  else
33	    log_result "Jest unit tests" "FAIL"
34	  fi
35	else
36	  echo "  [SKIP] No unit_tests/package.json found"
37	fi
38	
39	# ── API Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40	echo ""
41	echo "--- API Tests (curl) ---"
42	
43	BASE="http://localhost:3001"
44	
45	# Helper: assert HTTP status
46	assert_status() {
47	  local label="$1"
48	  local expected="$2"
49	  local actual="$3"
50	  if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
51	    log_result "$label" "PASS"
52	  else
53	    log_result "$label (expected $expected, got $actual)" "FAIL"
54	  fi
55	}
56	
57	# 1. Health / login
58	LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_login.json -w "%{http_code}" \
59	  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
60	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
61	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}')
62	assert_status "POST /auth/login (admin)" "201" "$LOGIN_RESP"
63	
64	ADMIN_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_login.json 2>/dev/null || true)
65	
66	# 2. Wrong password → 401
67	BAD_LOGIN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
68	  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
69	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
70	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrongpassword"}')
71	assert_status "POST /auth/login (bad password → 401)" "401" "$BAD_LOGIN"
72	
73	# 3. List listings (public)
74	LISTINGS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings")
75	assert_status "GET /listings (public)" "200" "$LISTINGS_STATUS"
76	
77	# 4. Get profile (authenticated)
78	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
79	  ME_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
80	    "$BASE/users/me" \
81	    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
82	  assert_status "GET /users/me (admin)" "200" "$ME_STATUS"
83	else
84	  log_result "GET /users/me (skipped — no token)" "FAIL"
85	fi
86	
87	# 5. Unauthenticated access to protected route
88	UNAUTH=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/users/me")
89	assert_status "GET /users/me (no token → 401)" "401" "$UNAUTH"
90	
91	# 6. Shopper login
92	SHOPPER_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_shopper.json -w "%{http_code}" \
93	  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
94	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/pet-market-operations-system/repo/run_tests.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PASS=0
FAIL=0

log_result() {
  local label="$1"
  local status="$2"
  if [ "$status" = "PASS" ]; then
    echo "  [PASS] $label"
    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
  else
    echo "  [FAIL] $label"
    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
  fi
}

echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  PetMarket Test Suite"
echo "========================================"

# ── Unit Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "--- Unit Tests (Jest) ---"
cd "$ROOT/unit_tests"
if [ -f package.json ]; then
  if npm test --silent 2>&1 | grep -q "PASS"; then
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "FAIL"
  fi
else
  echo "  [SKIP] No unit_tests/package.json found"
fi

# ── API Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "--- API Tests (curl) ---"

BASE="http://localhost:3001/api"

# Helper: assert HTTP status
assert_status() {
  local label="$1"
  local expected="$2"
  local actual="$3"
  if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
    log_result "$label" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "$label (expected $expected, got $actual)" "FAIL"
  fi
}

# 1. Login as admin
LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_login.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Device-Fingerprint: test-fp-admin" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin123"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/login (admin)" "201" "$LOGIN_RESP"

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_login.json 2>/dev/null || true)

# 2. Wrong password → 401
BAD_LOGIN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrongpassword"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/login (bad password → 401)" "401" "$BAD_LOGIN"

# 3. Register new user
REG_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_reg.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/register" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"testuser_'$$'","password":"testpass123","email":"test'$$'@example.com"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/register (new user)" "201" "$REG_STATUS"

# 4. List listings (public)
LISTINGS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings")
assert_status "GET /api/listings (public)" "200" "$LISTINGS_STATUS"

# 5. Search with query param
SEARCH_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings?q=golden&sort=price_asc")
assert_status "GET /api/listings?q=golden (search)" "200" "$SEARCH_STATUS"

# 6. Suggest
SUGGEST_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings/suggest?q=gol")
assert_status "GET /api/listings/suggest (autocomplete)" "200" "$SUGGEST_STATUS"

# 7. Get profile (authenticated)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  ME_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/users/me" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/users/me (admin)" "200" "$ME_STATUS"
else
  log_result "GET /api/users/me (skipped — no token)" "FAIL"
fi

# 8. Unauthenticated access to protected route
UNAUTH=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/users/me")
assert_status "GET /api/users/me (no token → 401)" "401" "$UNAUTH"

# 9. Shopper login
SHOPPER_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_shopper.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"shopper","password":"shopper123"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/login (shopper)" "201" "$SHOPPER_RESP"
SHOPPER_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_shopper.json 2>/dev/null || true)

# 10. Audit log (admin only)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  AUDIT_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/audit" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/audit (admin)" "200" "$AUDIT_STATUS"
fi

# 11. Audit log forbidden for shopper
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then
  AUDIT_FORBIDDEN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/audit" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/audit (shopper → 403)" "403" "$AUDIT_FORBIDDEN"
fi

# 12. Campaigns
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  CAMPS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/campaigns" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/campaigns (admin)" "200" "$CAMPS_STATUS"
fi

# 13. Credits (own)
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then
  CREDIT_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/credits/me" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/credits/me (shopper)" "200" "$CREDIT_STATUS"
fi

# 14. Vendor login + create listing
VENDOR_RESP=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_vendor.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"vendor","password":"vendor123"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/login (vendor)" "201" "$VENDOR_RESP"
VENDOR_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.data.token // empty' /tmp/pm_vendor.json 2>/dev/null || true)

if [ -n "$VENDOR_TOKEN" ]; then
  CREATE_LISTING=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_listing.json -w "%{http_code}" \
    -X POST "$BASE/listings" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $VENDOR_TOKEN" \
    -d '{"title":"Test Poodle Puppy","description":"Healthy and playful poodle","breed":"Poodle","age":3,"region":"Oregon","priceUsd":900}')
  assert_status "POST /api/listings (vendor create)" "201" "$CREATE_LISTING"
fi

# 15. Conversations — shopper starts conversation
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then
  LISTING_ID=$(curl -s "$BASE/listings" | jq -r '.data.items[0].id // empty' 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$LISTING_ID" ]; then
    CONV_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_conv.json -w "%{http_code}" \
      -X POST "$BASE/conversations" \
      -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHOPPER_TOKEN" \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d "{\"listingId\":\"$LISTING_ID\"}")
    assert_status "POST /api/conversations (shopper)" "201" "$CONV_STATUS"
  fi
fi

# 16. Canned responses (admin)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
  CANNED_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    "$BASE/conversations/canned-responses" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")
  assert_status "GET /api/conversations/canned-responses" "200" "$CANNED_STATUS"

  CREATE_CANNED=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -X POST "$BASE/admin/canned-responses" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
    -d '{"title":"Welcome","body":"Thank you for your interest!"}')
  assert_status "POST /api/admin/canned-responses (admin)" "201" "$CREATE_CANNED"
fi

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
echo "========================================"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi
exit 0
"}

  else

  if npm test --silent 2>&1 | grep -q "PASS"; then
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "Jest unit tests" "FAIL"
  fi

fi

echo "--- API Tests (curl) ---"

BASE="http://localhost:3001/api"

# Helper: assert HTTP status
assert_status() {
  local label="$1"
  local expected="$2"
  local actual="$3"
  if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
    log_result "$label" "PASS"
  else
    log_result "$label (expected $expected, got $actual)" "FAIL"
  fi
}

# 2. Wrong password → 401
BAD_LOGIN=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrongpassword"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/login (bad password → 401)" "401" "$BAD_LOGIN"

# 3. Register new user
REG_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pm_reg.json -w "%{http_code}" \
  -X POST "$BASE/auth/register" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"testuser_'$$'","password":"testpass123","email":"test'$$'@example.com"}')
assert_status "POST /api/auth/register (new user)" "201" "$REG_STATUS"

# 4. List listings (public)
LISTINGS_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings")
assert_status "GET /api/listings (public)" "200" "$LISTINGS_STATUS"

# 5. Search with query param
SEARCH_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/listings?q=golden&sort=price_asc")
assert_status "GET /api/listings?q=golden (search)" "200" "$SEARCH_STATUS"

# 6. Suggest

assert_status "GET /api/listings/suggest (autocomplete)" "200" "$SUGGEST_STATUS"

# 7. Get profile (authenticated)
if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then

    "$BASE/users/me" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN")

else

fi

# 8. Unauthenticated access to protected route
UNAUTH=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE/users/me")
assert_status "GET /api/users/me (no token → 401)" "401" "$UNAUTH"

# 9. Shopper login

if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then

if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then

fi

# 15. Conversations — shopper starts conversation
if [ -n "$SHOPPER_TOKEN" ]; then

Continue the `petmarket` NestJS backend. Implement the remaining backend modules fully. Same standards: no mocks, full TypeORM, DTOs, guards, audit logging.

## 1. Settlement & Freight Engine (`/api/settlements`)

**Freight calculation** — `FreightService.calculate(params)`:
- Inputs: `distanceMiles`, `weightLbs`, `dimWeightLbs` (L×W×H/139), `isOversized`, `isWeekend`
- billableWeight = max(weightLbs, dimWeightLbs)
- baseCost: 0-50mi → $5, 51-200mi → $12, 201-500mi → $25, 500+mi → $45
- perPoundRate: $0.08/lb for billableWeight
- oversizedSurcharge: isOversized ? +$15.00 : 0
- weekendSurcharge: isWeekend ? totalBeforeTax * 0.05 : 0
- salesTax: 8.5% of (base + per-pound + surcharges)
- Return itemized breakdown object

**Monthly Statements**:
- `POST /api/settlements/generate-monthly`: admin only. Generates statements for all vendors for the given `{ month: "YYYY-MM" }`. One statement per vendor. Calculates totalCharges by summing freight estimates from that month's listings. Includes taxAmount. Status = pending.
- `GET /api/settlements`: admin sees all, vendor sees own. Filter by month, status.
- `GET /api/settlements/:id`: detail with variance reconciliation: `{ expected, actual, variance, variancePercent }`.
- `POST /api/settlements/:id/approve`: two-step approval:
  - Step 1: role=ops_reviewer sets status → reviewer_approved
  - Step 2: role=finance_admin sets status → finance_approved
  - Wrong role attempting wrong step returns 403 with clear message.
- `POST /api/settlements/:id/reject`: either approver role, sets status → rejected, requires `reason`.
- `GET /api/settlements/export/:id`: streams CSV of settlement detail. Adds watermark header row: `"CONFIDENTIAL - {username} - {timestamp}"`. Phone numbers masked to last 4 digits, addresses truncated to city+state for non-admin.

## 2. Campaigns & Content Config (`/api/admin/campaigns`)
- `POST /api/admin/campaigns`: admin. Fields: title, type(announcement/carousel/recommendation), startTime, endTime, slotIndex(1-10 for carousel), data(jsonb), status(draft/active).
- `GET /api/admin/campaigns`: list with filter by type, status, date range.
- `PUT /api/admin/campaigns/:id`: update. Validate startTime < endTime.
- `DELETE /api/admin/campaigns/:id`: soft delete.
- `GET /api/campaigns/active`: public endpoint. Returns currently active campaigns where `now BETWEEN startTime AND endTime`.
- `POST /api/admin/sensitive-words`: add word to sensitive_words table. Admin only.
- `DELETE /api/admin/sensitive-words/:id`: remove.
- `GET /api/admin/sensitive-words`: list all.

## 3. Risk & Anomaly Detection (middleware + service)
`RiskService` — called on listing create and conversation create:
- **Multi-account signal**: if same deviceFingerprint exists on >2 different userId in users table → flag, log to audit, add to response as `{ riskFlag: "multi_account_device" }`.
- **IP risk**: if same IP created >5 accounts in last 24h (check users table) → flag similarly.
- **Frequent repost**: if vendor posted same-breed listing >5 times in last 1 hour → flag as `{ riskFlag: "frequent_repost" }`.
- Risk flags do NOT block the action but are included in response and logged in audit_logs with action=`RISK_FLAG`.

## 4. Data Export Engine (`/api/exports`)
- `POST /api/exports/jobs`: admin only. Body: `{ type: listings|conversations|settlements|audit, filters: {...} }`. Creates export_job with status=queued. Returns jobId immediately.
- Concurrency: max 2 jobs with status=running at any time. If at limit, new job stays queued.
- Background processor: `ExportProcessorService` using `setInterval` (every 5s) picks up queued jobs (respecting concurrency limit), runs query with filters, writes CSV to `/tmp/exports/{jobId}.csv`, updates status=done, sets expiresAt = now + 7 days.
- Permission-based masking in CSV: non-admin requesters get phone last-4 only, addresses masked.
- `GET /api/exports/jobs`: list requester's jobs with status, progress(%), createdAt, expiresAt.
- `GET /api/exports/jobs/:id/download`: streams file if status=done and not expired. Returns 404 if expired, 202 if still processing.
- Auto-expire: processor also marks jobs as expired if expiresAt < now.

## 5. Audit Console (`/api/admin/audit`)
- `GET /api/admin/audit`: admin only. Filters: actorId, entityType, action, startDate, endDate, keyword(searches before/after jsonb as text). Paginated. Returns logs with actor username resolved.
- `GET /api/admin/audit/:id/verify`: verifies hash chain integrity for a single log entry — recomputes SHA-256(prevHash + entry data) and compares to stored hash. Returns `{ valid: boolean, entry }`.
- `POST /api/admin/audit/export`: creates export job of type=audit with filters. Returns jobId.

## 6. Power User Query (`/api/query`)
- `POST /api/query`: admin/vendor. Body: `{ entity: listings|users|conversations|settlements, filters: [{field, op: eq|gt|lt|gte|lte|contains|in, value}], sort: {field, dir}, page, limit }`. Dynamically builds TypeORM QueryBuilder from filters. Returns paginated results with masking applied. Saved lists: `POST /api/query/save { name, params }`, `GET /api/query/saved`, `DELETE /api/query/saved/:id`.

Output complete file content for all new/modified files.

69 files. All 6 modules implemented. Here's a summary of everything created/modified:

### New files (16):
- `settlements/freight.service.ts` — tiered freight calculator with distance/weight/oversized/weekend/tax
- `settlements/dto/settlement.dto.ts` — GenerateMonthlyDto, FreightCalcDto, RejectDto, SettlementFiltersDto
- `campaigns/dto/campaign.dto.ts` — CreateCampaignDto (slotIndex 1-10, startTime < endTime), UpdateCampaignDto, CampaignFiltersDto, SensitiveWordDto
- `risk/risk.service.ts` — multi-account device detection, IP risk, frequent repost flagging; logs to audit, returns flags in response
- `risk/risk.module.ts`
- `exports/dto/export.dto.ts` — CreateExportJobDto (type: listings|conversations|settlements|audit)
- `query/dto/query.dto.ts` — PowerQueryDto (dynamic filters with eq/gt/lt/gte/lte/contains/in), SaveQueryDto
- `query/query.service.ts` — builds TypeORM QueryBuilder dynamically from filters, whitelisted fields per entity, vendor scoping, saved queries CRUD
- `query/query.controller.ts` — POST /query, POST /query/save, GET /query/saved, DELETE /query/saved/:id
- `query/query.module.ts`
- `database/entities/saved-query.entity.ts` — for saved query persistence

### Modified files (12):
- **app.module.ts** — added SavedQuery entity, RiskModule, QueryModule
- **campaign.entity.ts** — added DeleteDateColumn for soft delete
- **settlements/settlements.service.ts** — full rewrite: `generateMonthly()` sums freight per vendor, `findOne()` with variance reconciliation, `reject()` with reason, `exportCsv()` with watermark + data masking, filter support
- **settlements/settlements.controller.ts** — full rewrite: added generate-monthly, freight/calculate, unified approve endpoint, reject, export CSV streaming
- **settlements/settlements.module.ts** — imports Listing, User, FreightService, EncryptionService
- **campaigns/campaigns.service.ts** — full rewrite: filtered findAll, findActive (public), create/update with startTime<endTime validation, soft delete, sensitive words CRUD with audit
- **campaigns/campaigns.controller.ts** — split into CampaignsPublicController (GET /campaigns/active), AdminCampaignsController (CRUD), AdminSensitiveWordsController
- **campaigns/campaigns.module.ts** — imports SensitiveWord, AuditModule, registers all 3 controllers
- **exports/exports.service.ts** — full rewrite: background processor with setInterval, max 2 concurrent, CSV generation per type, auto-expire, permission-based masking
- **exports/exports.controller.ts** — rewritten: POST /exports/jobs, GET /exports/jobs, GET /exports/jobs/:id, GET /exports/jobs/:id/download (file stream)
- **exports/exports.module.ts** — added EncryptionService
- **audit/audit.service.ts** — added `findAllFiltered()` with actorId/entityType/action/date/keyword filters + actor username resolution, `verifyEntry()` for hash chain integrity check
- **audit/audit.controller.ts** — added AdminAuditController with filtered search, hash verify endpoint, audit export
- **audit/audit.module.ts** — imports User entity, registers AdminAuditController

Build the complete `petmarket` React frontend using React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui. This is a production-grade, premium UI. Every page must be fully functional, connected to the real backend API at `http://localhost:3001`, responsive (mobile/tablet/desktop), and visually polished.

## Design System
- Color palette: primary #1a56db (blue), success #0e9f6e, warning #e3a008, danger #f05252, neutral grays. Dark sidebar, white content area.
- Typography: Inter font (Google Fonts). Headings bold, body regular.
- Components: use shadcn/ui for cards, dialogs, tables, badges, dropdowns, toasts. Augment with custom Tailwind.
- All tables: sortable columns headers with arrow icons, row hover highlight, sticky header.
- All forms: React Hook Form + Zod validation. Show field-level errors inline. Submit button shows spinner + disabled state during request.
- All async data: React Query with loading skeleton (shimmer effect) and error state with retry button.
- Toast notifications (top-right) for all success/error API responses.

## Pages to implement fully:

### 1. `/login`
Clean centered card. Username + password fields. "Sign in" button. On success: store token+user in Zustand, redirect by role (admin→/admin/config, vendor→/listings, shopper→/listings). On error: show inline error message.

### 2. `/listings` (Shopper + Vendor view)
**Left sidebar filters panel** (collapsible on mobile):
- Keyword search input with 300ms debounce → calls `/api/listings/suggest` for autocomplete dropdown (10 suggestions)
- Breed select (populated from unique breeds in listings)
- Region select
- Age range dual-handle slider
- Price range (USD) dual-handle slider
- Min rating star selector (1-5)
- "New Arrivals only" toggle (last 14 days)
- Sort by: dropdown (Newest, Price ↑, Price ↓, Rating)
- "Apply Filters" + "Reset" buttons

**Main grid**: responsive card grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 3-4 desktop). Each card: photo, title, breed badge, age, region, price in bold, star rating, "New" badge if <14 days old. Click → `/listings/:id`.

**No-results state**: show friendly illustration + "No listings found" + fallback section "Similar Breeds" (horizontal scroll cards) + "Trending This Week" grid.

**Vendor additions**: "+ New Listing" button → modal form (title, description, breed, age, region, price, photos URLs). If API returns `flagged:true`, show yellow warning banner "Listing submitted for review — prohibited terms detected."

**Pagination**: page number controls at bottom with total count display.

### 3. `/listings/:id`
Two-column layout (desktop): left = listing details (full description, all photos in gallery carousel, freight estimate calculator widget), right = conversation panel (see below).

**Freight Estimate Widget**: inputs for distance(mi), weight(lbs), dimensions (L×W×H), weekend checkbox. "Calculate" → POST to `/api/settlements/freight-estimate` → show itemized breakdown table (base, per-pound, oversized, weekend, tax, total). Animate total with count-up effect.

### 4. `/conversations`
Split-pane layout: left = conversation list (searchable by keyword, filterable by date range, archived toggle, shows listing title + last message preview + unread dot), right = active conversation.

**Active conversation panel**:
- Message thread: text bubbles (left=other, right=self). Internal notes shown with yellow background + lock icon (vendor/admin only).
- Voice note messages: show audio player (HTML5 `<audio>` element with controls).
- Message input: textarea + send button + paperclip icon for voice upload (file input, accepts audio/*) + "Insert Canned Response" dropdown.
- "Add Internal Note" toggle button (vendor/admin only).
- Top bar: listing title, shopper name, "Archive" button.

### 5. `/admin/config` (Admin only)
Tabbed interface:
- **Campaigns tab**: table of campaigns with type badge, start/end dates, status. "New Campaign" button → modal: title, type (announcement/carousel/recommendation), slot (1-10 for carousel), start/end datetime pickers, data textarea (JSON), status toggle. Edit/delete actions per row.
- **Sensitive Words tab**: tag-cloud display of current words + input to add new + delete (×) on each tag. Confirm dialog before delete.
- **Canned Responses tab**: table with title+body preview. Add/edit modal. Delete with confirm.
- **Announcements**: current active announcements previewed as banner mockup.

### 6. `/admin/settlements`
Table of all settlements: vendor name, month, totalCharges, taxAmount, status badge (colored: pending=gray, reviewer_approved=blue, finance_approved=green, rejected=red), actions.
- "Generate Monthly" button (admin) → date-month picker → confirm → call API.
- Row expand → show variance reconciliation: expected vs actual as mini bar chart.
- Approve/Reject buttons appear based on user's exact role (ops_reviewer or finance_admin). Disable if step not applicable.
- Export button → download CSV.

### 7. `/admin/audit`
Filter bar: actor, entity type, action, date range pickers, keyword search.
Results table: timestamp, actor, action, entity, before/after (collapsible JSON diff viewer). Paginated.
"Verify Integrity" button per row → calls verify endpoint → show green checkmark or red warning.
"Export Audit Log" button → creates export job → shows job ID toast.

### 8. `/admin/exports`
Job queue table: columns = type, status (badge with icon), created, expires, progress bar, download button (enabled only when done+not expired). Auto-refreshes every 5s via React Query `refetchInterval`.
"New Export" button → modal: entity select, filter fields by entity type, submit → shows new queued job immediately.

### 9. `/admin/query` (Power Query)
Dynamic filter builder: "+ Add Filter" button adds a row with [field dropdown] [operator dropdown] [value input]. Multiple filters combined.
Entity selector tabs at top.
Sort by field + direction.
"Run Query" → paginated results table with sortable columns.
"Save Query" button → name input → saves. "Saved Queries" sidebar with load/delete.
Results table has "Export" button → creates export job.

## Global Components
- `<AppLayout>`: sidebar + topbar. Sidebar shows role-appropriate nav items with icons. Active route highlighted. Collapsible on mobile (hamburger).
- `<ProtectedRoute>`: wraps pages, checks auth + role, redirects to /login if needed.
